steps.title: Use the completed main clause
steps.content: The main clause already has its subject and verb: Her broad singing repertoire lent Fairuz a timeless appeal. After the comma, the blank should add a modifying phrase showing the result of that appeal.

steps.title: Reject finite verbs
steps.content: A and B are finite verb forms, which would create a comma splice after the complete main clause. D is an ungrammatical base form here. A present participle forms a result phrase that attaches smoothly to the main clause. The answer is C.
